decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequences from 340 isolates were used to reconstruct the evolutionary history, geographic spread, and transmission of the ST772 lineage.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic reconstruction of an infectious-pathogen lineage and its international transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study reanalyzed Ebola sequences to quantify how using a single gene instead of full genomes changes phylogenetic, temporal, and spatial inference resolution.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance assessment of sequence-data resolution for outbreak inference.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The article reviews genetics-based research and discusses potential tools for elimination decisions, but does not introduce, apply, critique, validate, correct, or implement a specific transmission-reconstruction method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit review and programmatic guidance on using genomic information to distinguish local persistence from parasite reintroduction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing was compared with MLVA for outbreak inference, and the study identified low-diversity conditions in which genomic epizootiological and phylodynamic methods have limited utility.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of genomic resolution and limitations for reconstructing transmission in a slowly evolving pathogen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors analyzed local and global Vibrio cholerae genomes to infer origins, repeated introductions, and regional and international spread routes for two lineages.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application reconstructing pathogen lineage dissemination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing, phylogenetic classification, and genome-based clustering were used to identify tuberculosis transmission clusters and an MDR transmission network.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to tuberculosis transmission in eastern Sudan.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Short- and long-read genome sequencing was used to characterize multispecies dissemination of blaKPC through lineages, transposons, and plasmid exchange.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic investigation of transmissible carbapenem resistance dissemination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: SARS-CoV-2 genomes were analyzed with coalescent exponential-growth and birth-death skyline models to estimate evolutionary dynamics and the reproduction number.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogenetic application to an emerging infectious-disease outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome and long-read sequencing were used to characterize resistant Klebsiella clonal groups and the spread-associated plasmid background in Serbia.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to resistant clinical isolates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Nationwide gonococcal genomes were assigned to sequence types and Bayesian clusters and used to examine demographic transmission patterns and reinfections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology analysis of gonorrhoea cases.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latin American genomes were combined with international genomes to reconstruct the global VREfm population structure and date major lineage splits.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogenomic reconstruction of a resistant pathogen population.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Clinical Campylobacter genomes were analyzed with broader US data to characterize lineage diversity, geographic mixing, recombination, and resistance evolution.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology study of circulating clinical pathogen lineages.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing, phylogenetic associations, and phylodynamic dating were used to identify and date persistent Brucella abortus lineages and introductions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic and phylodynamic reconstruction of pathogen lineage persistence and spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: HIV pol sequences were analyzed using phylodynamics, genetic-distance networks, and global phylogenies to identify transmission clusters and repeated introductions into Bulgaria.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete molecular reconstruction of HIV transmission patterns.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing and bioinformatics were used retrospectively to identify a single clone and rapid spread during a biphasic Streptococcus outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology investigation of an institutional outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: HIV sequences were analyzed with phylogenetic clustering and a birth-death skyline model to estimate transmission changes following the TRIP intervention.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylodynamic application evaluating changes in an HIV epidemic.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing and epidemiological data were integrated to construct a transmission map and identify the initial case and superspreader in a neonatal-unit outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic reconstruction of a nosocomial transmission route.

decisions.relationship_evidence: SARS-CoV-2 genomes were combined with epidemiological and phylogenetic analyses to infer introductions and putative local transmission chains in Guangdong.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to early COVID-19 transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Patient data and whole-genome sequencing were integrated to characterize MRSA population changes and identify genomic clusters potentially related to healthcare transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology analysis of hospitalized MRSA cases.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Ukrainian gonococcal isolates underwent WGS-based epidemiology and phylogenomic analysis to identify circulating clusters, lineages, and resistance determinants.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to gonococcal spread and resistance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Phylogenetic analysis of Northern California SARS-CoV-2 genomes identified at least seven introductions and transmission associated with outbreak clusters.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology reconstruction of viral introductions and local spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Local and public Citrobacter genomes were compared phylogenetically to characterize population diversity and carbapenemase-plasmid relationships.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete clinical and genomic epidemiology analysis of resistant pathogens.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequences from Thailand and public data were analyzed to estimate the origin, historical spread, and population trajectory of tilapia lake virus.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology reconstruction of an emerging infectious virus.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A genomic epidemiology approach was used to characterize local and global population structure and phylogenetic lineages of E. coli O145.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic application to a foodborne pathogen population.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper identifies nonadditivity and biased uncertainty in existing relaxed-clock models and proposes a new additive relaxed-clock model for dating genomic epidemiology phylogenies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development illustrated on simulated and real datasets.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing and SNP-based clustering were used to characterize tuberculosis diversity, resistance, and ongoing transmission in southern Brazil.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application identifying active TB transmission clusters.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Genomic analysis of animal-derived E. coli was used to infer recent regional dissemination and independent acquisition of plasmid-encoded tet(X4).
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology study of antimicrobial-resistance spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Campylobacter genomes from a pediatric cohort were contextualized globally to infer likely poultry sources and locally unusual infection genotypes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to pediatric Campylobacter infection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing and phylogenomics were used to define gonococcal lineages, sublineages, networks, and resistance determinants across Brazil.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete national genomic epidemiology analysis of gonorrhoea.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The article recommends genomic-hub and portable-sequencing capacity for African outbreak response but does not itself introduce, apply, critique, validate, correct, or implement a transmission-reconstruction method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit strategic guidance on genomic infrastructure and outbreak-surveillance capacity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Gonococcal genomes, susceptibility data, and patient data were used to reconstruct phylogeny and define transmission clusters across demographic groups in New York City.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology analysis of local gonorrhoea transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: HIV pol sequences were analyzed with phylogenetic clustering, BEAST2 phylodynamics, and a structured coalescent to infer transmission dynamics among people who inject drugs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylodynamic reconstruction of an HIV epidemic in Pakistan.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A time-scaled phylogeny of Salmonella genomes was used to date the clone's origin and infer introductions and subsequent spread between North Africa and Europe.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology reconstruction of resistant-clone dissemination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: RSV genomes sampled over two epidemics were used to characterize introductions, local transmission, diversification, and seasonal evolutionary dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to respiratory-virus transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: This is a review of molecular epidemiological and nucleic-acid diagnostic advances and does not itself perform an allowed methodological relationship role.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit review guidance concerning genomic epidemiology and COVID-19 diagnostics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The essay reflects historically on microbial genomic epidemiology and its uses but does not introduce, apply, critique, validate, correct, or implement a specific method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Field-level perspective describing the development and prospective value of microbial genomic epidemiology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The report lays out a data-infrastructure framework for connecting viral sequences with clinical and epidemiological data but does not perform an allowed paper-to-concept role.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit framework and coordination guidance for genomic epidemiology data infrastructure.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Dense patient and environmental genome surveillance was analyzed with clustering, networks, and an SNP cutoff to quantify hospital acquisition and transmission of E. faecium.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic reconstruction of nosocomial pathogen transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Phenotypic and whole-genome data from farms, chicken carcasses, and humans were compared to characterize related Salmonella Infantis strains across the food-human interface.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to a foodborne pathogen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper is a review of global Streptococcus pyogenes population genomics and how genomics informs transmission and vaccine design, without performing an allowed relationship role itself.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit global review of genomic epidemiology evidence and interpretation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing of isolates from three hospitals was used to identify lineages, predominant clones, and persistent within- and between-hospital transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology investigation of a nosocomial epidemic.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Two web-based WGS platforms and multiple reference genomes were evaluated for concordance with epidemiologically defined Clostridium perfringens outbreaks and for identifying outliers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit validation of genomic clustering performance across analysis platforms.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper presents PathoSPOT, a toolkit automating integration of genomic and medical-record data for rapid detection and tracing of nosocomial outbreaks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit software implementation demonstrated on MRSA surveillance data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Colombian SARS-CoV-2 genomes were integrated with epidemiological, travel, and phylogenetic observations to reconstruct introductions and linked transmission chains.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic reconstruction of early COVID-19 spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Deep viral sequencing and epidemiological surveillance were combined to reconstruct superspreading events and tourism-related SARS-CoV-2 dissemination.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology reconstruction of viral transmission events.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Influenza genome sequencing, surveillance, and electronic records were combined in real time to trace a nosocomial outbreak to a single source patient.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application during an active hospital outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing established genetic relatedness among nontoxigenic C. diphtheriae cases and supported an outbreak-consistent cluster.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology investigation of linked infections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Clinical E. coli genomes were analyzed phylogenetically to characterize expansion and geographic relatedness of resistant lineages in Malawi.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete longitudinal genomic epidemiology application using locally generated sequencing data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analysis of 772 SARS-CoV-2 genomes identified introductions and contrasted the transmission reach and genetic variation of two superspreading events.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology analysis of community and institutional spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: New Zealand SARS-CoV-2 genomes were used to infer introductions, onward transmission, and changes in the effective reproduction number after lockdown.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application assessing epidemic transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study develops and proposes whole- and core-genome SNP cutoffs above which recent MRSA patient-to-patient transmission can be excluded.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development using three estimation approaches and two cohorts.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing and bioinformatics characterized Klebsiella population structure, genomic clusters, resistance elements, and clonal dissemination in a hospital.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology analysis of carbapenemase-producing clinical strains.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Italian SARS-CoV-2 genomes and global sequences were analyzed phylogenetically to identify introductions, regional transmission clusters, and persistence through lockdown.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology reconstruction of Italy's first epidemic wave.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Contact tracing and SARS-CoV-2 genomes were combined to identify viral lineages, contact clusters, multiple introductions, and local transmission in Karnataka.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete integrated genomic epidemiology outbreak investigation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Long-term surveillance and full-length CV-A16 genomes were analyzed to infer clusters, geographic diffusion, transmission patterns, and recombinant-form changes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology reconstruction of viral dissemination in China.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Phylogenetic and epidemiological analysis of 1,314 SARS-CoV-2 genomes estimated hundreds of introductions into Scotland and identified early community and healthcare outbreaks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology reconstruction of importation and transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing and phylogenetic analysis were used to identify two outbreak lineages and estimate ancestry, evolutionary rate, and reproduction number in Hong Kong.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic reconstruction of early SARS-CoV-2 transmission dynamics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Sequencing of influenza viruses was used to investigate emergence, introduction, cocirculation, and outward spread of highly pathogenic A/H7N9 in Guangdong.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to avian-influenza origins and dissemination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome data from wild-animal E. coli were analyzed to assess population structure, host relationships, and evidence for recent transmission to livestock or humans.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology study of potential zoonotic reservoirs and transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole SARS-CoV-2 genomes were used to identify seven lineages and local transmission clusters showing sustained community spread in Lombardy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology reconstruction of early regional transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: HCV sequencing and phylogenetic linkage analysis identified transmission clusters across correctional facilities and syringe-service settings.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to HCV transmission networks.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Moroccan and global SARS-CoV-2 genomes were analyzed phylogenetically to infer multiple introductions and cryptic circulation before the first detected case.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology reconstruction of viral introduction and spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The article provides protocols for using the TransPhylo R package to simulate and infer transmission trees from dated pathogen phylogenies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit software implementation and usage protocols for transmission reconstruction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing and phylogenetic analysis tested whether four Cryptococcus cases shared a source and showed that all isolates were genetically distinct.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology investigation preserving a null linkage finding.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Prospective population-based genomic surveillance identified clustered and unique tuberculosis isolates and assessed contributions from local transmission, travel, and incarceration.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic analysis of TB transmission at an international border.

decisions.relationship_evidence: SARS-CoV-2 genomic and temporal data were integrated to distinguish care-home outbreaks from independent introductions with limited onward transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to transmission in care homes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: SARS-CoV-2 genomes and phylodynamic analysis were used to characterize circulating lineages and infer multiple entries into Madrid before restrictions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology analysis of the first pandemic wave.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Brazilian and global viral genomes were analyzed to reconstruct the origin and dissemination of lineage B.1.1.33 and estimate intervention-associated changes in its reproduction number.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylodynamic reconstruction of SARS-CoV-2 lineage spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing and phylogenetics characterized four MRSA clades, their geographic dissemination, and their relationships to global epidemic clones.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology study of circulating MRSA in Argentina.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Densely sampled SARS-CoV-2 genomes were analyzed with Bayesian phylodynamics to estimate reproduction numbers and infections through time for a localized outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology analysis of a geographically constrained outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study creates a pathogen genome identity score and integrates it with instantaneous reproduction-number estimates and epidemic curves to connect genomic variation with outbreak dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for integrated genomic and epidemiological outbreak tracking.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome SNP analysis of clinical and environmental Candida auris isolates identified hospital transmission patterns and a South Asian lineage origin.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology investigation of fungal transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The systematic review benchmarks tuberculosis genomic epidemiology reports against STROME-ID criteria and quantifies substantial reporting and reproducibility deficiencies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of reporting quality across 114 genomic epidemiology studies.
named_source_mentions: Strengthening the Reporting of Molecular Epidemiology for Infectious Diseases (STROME-ID) guidelines

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors implemented an end-to-end Ebola genomic-surveillance system covering sequencing, bioinformatics, and dissemination of genomic results to response workers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit operational implementation accompanied by phylodynamic analysis of outbreak genomes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper presents and implements a public-health genomic epidemiology scheme for N. gonorrhoeae in Pathogenwatch, including typing, resistance prediction, pipelines, and a public genome collection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit platform and bioinformatics implementation for genomic epidemiology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Clinical SARS-CoV-2 genomes were analyzed to characterize clade changes and associate viral variants with disease severity and patient outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to an initial pandemic wave.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Genomic epidemiology, incidence data, and phylodynamics were integrated to infer introductions, domestic transmission, and reproduction-number changes in Belarus.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic reconstruction of national SARS-CoV-2 transmission dynamics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Tuberculosis genomes and epidemiological data were used to characterize lineages, date clades, and identify transmission clusters spanning communities and years.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to TB transmission in eastern Malaysia.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome sequencing and dated phylogenetics reconstructed the emergence and intra- and interfacility spread of NDM-producing Klebsiella across post-acute-care facilities.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology investigation of a regional healthcare outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: SARS-CoV-2 genomes were analyzed with phylogenetic and phylodynamic methods to identify introductions, related clusters, and spatial spread in southern Brazil.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application at municipality scale.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Three years of Candida auris isolates underwent whole-genome phylogenetic and population-structure analysis to infer local subclade relationships and origin.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology surveillance study of an emerging fungal pathogen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: SARS-CoV-2 genomes were used to reconstruct successive lineage replacements and the emergence and dissemination of the P.1 variant during two epidemic waves.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology reconstruction of regional COVID-19 dynamics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study explicitly evaluates the feasibility of neighborhood- and building-scale wastewater sequencing for tracking SARS-CoV-2 strains and other pathogens.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Feasibility validation of a wastewater-based genomic epidemiology approach.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Phylogenetic analysis and person-place links were combined to construct a potential transmission network for a COVID-19 group-home outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to a congregate-setting outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: SNV, plasmid, and epidemiological analyses were integrated to confirm, expand, merge, and exclude hospital transmission clusters relative to conventional epidemiology.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology investigation of healthcare transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Mainland Chinese SARS-CoV-2 genomes were analyzed using phylogenetic and time-scaled methods to estimate ancestry, evolutionary rate, and genetic diversity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to the Chinese outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulated epidemics with direct and environmental transmission were reconstructed using SCOTTI to evaluate how diversity, transmission route, and pathogen ecology affect phylodynamic accuracy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation-based assessment of transmission-tree reconstruction performance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Clinical Staphylococcus aureus genomes were analyzed to date major MRSA clones and characterize their expansion, recombination, and changing population sizes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology analysis of bloodstream-infection isolates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Palestinian SARS-CoV-2 genomes were placed in the global phylogeny to infer independent introductions, a predominant local lineage, and circulation predating detection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology reconstruction of early epidemic spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Typing, whole-genome sequencing, and SNP phylogenetics were used to characterize clonal outbreaks and multiregional spread of metallo-β-lactamase-producing Pseudomonas in Poland.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete molecular and genomic epidemiology study of resistant pathogen dissemination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome and phylogenetic analyses compared two C. difficile strains and inferred different reliance on healthcare versus community spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multisite genomic epidemiology analysis of strain-dependent transmission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors set up an integrated genomic-surveillance system combining sequencing, genetic cluster detection, contact tracing, and a dashboard, then used it to identify transmission chains.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit implementation and operational application of an integrated surveillance platform.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole genomes from cattle, beef, and humans were analyzed for phylogenomic backgrounds and expanding STEC clones across the livestock-food-human interface.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to zoonotic foodborne strains.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Meta-transcriptome sequencing was used to characterize SARS-CoV-2 introductions, local subclades, cross-settlement transmission, and viral evolution in the UAE.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology reconstruction of viral spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Sequencing, genomic clustering, transmission networks, epidemiological integration, and phylodynamics traced Victoria's second COVID-19 wave to a single quarantine incursion.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete large-scale genomic epidemiology reconstruction informing outbreak response.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Large-scale SARS-CoV-2 whole-genome sequencing and phylodynamic analysis tracked circulating variants and changes in viral spread over time.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic-surveillance application to regional viral dynamics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study evaluates accessory-genome variation alongside core-genome phylogeny and demonstrates its utility for resolving short-scale bacterial transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit assessment of accessory-genome information for genomic epidemiology performance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors developed a nine-level multilevel genome typing scheme for S. Enteritidis and demonstrated its use for surveillance, outbreak detection, and clone tracking.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development with an accompanying open database.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Whole-genome phylogenetics and outbreak investigations mapped SARS-CoV-2 transmission within and between long-term-care facilities and identified staff-mediated spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology reconstruction of congregate-facility outbreaks.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Genome sequencing and phylogenetic analysis were used to characterize geographic transmission of multidrug-resistant invasive Salmonella lineages across African countries.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic epidemiology application to international pathogen transmission.
